steps.title: Find the symbol-over-actions claim
steps.content: The student criticizes historians who evaluate Lumumba mainly as a symbol rather than through his actions. The quotation must show that exact contrast.

steps.title: Reject quotes without that contrast
steps.content:
• Choice A: It says Lumumba is hard to evaluate, not that he is treated mainly as a symbol.
  • Choice B: It concerns consistency in his beliefs.
  • Choice D: It concerns unanswered questions about his vision.

steps.title: Use the symbolic-personification quote
steps.content: The quotation that passes over practical accomplishments and emphasizes Lumumba as the personification of Congolese independence illustrates the student's criticism. The answer is C.
